foodItems.name: Apple
foodItems.educationalFact: Apples release ethylene gas that can make other produce spoil faster. Store separately!

foodItems.name: Banana
foodItems.educationalFact: Bananas ripen quickly. Freeze overripe ones for smoothies!

foodItems.name: Milk
foodItems.educationalFact: Milk stays freshest on the middle shelf, not the door!

foodItems.name: Yogurt
foodItems.educationalFact: Yogurt often stays good past the date on the package. Use your senses!

foodItems.name: Chicken Breast
foodItems.educationalFact: Raw chicken must be cooked within 1-2 days or frozen immediately.

foodItems.name: Ground Beef
foodItems.educationalFact: Ground meat spoils faster than whole cuts. Use quickly or freeze!

foodItems.name: Salmon
foodItems.educationalFact: Fresh fish should smell like the ocean, not fishy. Cook within 1 day!

foodItems.name: Lettuce
foodItems.educationalFact: Keep lettuce in a crisper drawer with a damp paper towel.

foodItems.name: Tomato
foodItems.educationalFact: Store tomatoes at room temperature for best flavor until ripe.

foodItems.name: Bread
foodItems.educationalFact: Freeze bread you won't use within a week - it stays fresh!

foodItems.name: Pasta
foodItems.educationalFact: Dry pasta lasts over a year in the pantry. Great for meal planning!

foodItems.name: Rice
foodItems.educationalFact: Uncooked rice can last years if kept dry and pest-free.

foodItems.name: Eggs
foodItems.educationalFact: Eggs can last 3-5 weeks past their carton date if refrigerated.

foodItems.name: Cheese
foodItems.educationalFact: Hard cheeses last longer than soft. Wrap tightly to prevent drying.

foodItems.name: Carrot
foodItems.educationalFact: Carrots last longest when stored in water in the fridge.

foodItems.name: Broccoli
foodItems.educationalFact: Keep broccoli in the crisper, loosely wrapped. Use stalks too!

foodItems.name: Potato
foodItems.educationalFact: Store potatoes in a cool, dark place - not the fridge!

foodItems.name: Onion
foodItems.educationalFact: Onions last longest in a cool, dry, well-ventilated area.

foodItems.name: Canned Beans
foodItems.educationalFact: Canned goods can last 2+ years. Great for emergency meals!

foodItems.name: Frozen Peas
foodItems.educationalFact: Frozen vegetables are picked at peak ripeness and retain nutrients.

foodItems.name: Strawberries
foodItems.educationalFact: Berries are very delicate. Don't wash until ready to eat!

foodItems.name: Spinach
foodItems.educationalFact: Leafy greens wilt fast. Store in sealed container with paper towel.

foodItems.name: Orange Juice
foodItems.educationalFact: Fresh OJ lasts about a week. Freeze in ice cube trays for smoothies!

foodItems.name: Leftovers
foodItems.educationalFact: Leftovers should be eaten within 3-4 days. Label with date!
